touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempted to Kwame Ahmed at SouthpointGateway. No answer, left voicemail introducing our platform's onboarding automation capabilities for EdTech companies.
LinkedIn connection request sent to Kwame Ahmed with personalized note about helping EdTech sales teams streamline onboarding processes. No response received.
Follow-up LinkedIn message referencing SouthpointGateway's recent growth and how similar mid-market EdTech companies have reduced onboarding time by 40%. No response.
Cold email sent highlighting case study from comparable EdTech company that automated their customer onboarding workflow. No response received.
Follow-up email with video demo link showing onboarding automation features specifically relevant to EdTech sales teams. No response.

Qualification call with Kwame Ahmed. He expressed interest after seeing our emails and mentioned current manual onboarding is taking 2-3 weeks per customer. Discussed team size and current pain points. Kwame mentioned they're looking at solutions but no urgency conveyed.

questions_asked.question: What's driving you to look at onboarding automation now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling fast and our manual process is breaking down. Takes too long to get customers live.

questions_asked.question: How many people are involved in your onboarding process currently?
questions_asked.answer: About 8-10 across sales and customer success, but we're hiring more.

next_steps: Kwame to share current onboarding workflow document
Schedule follow-up to discuss technical requirements

touches.ai_summary: Brief qualification follow-up with Kwame. He confirmed budget exists for this initiative but mentioned they're also talking to other vendors. Timeline discussed - looking to make decision within 2 months.

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Gong for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: Our automations focus is different from Gong's conversation intelligence - we can walk through a detailed comparison and pricing structure on our next call.

next_steps: Send pricing framework document
Schedule discovery call with broader team

touches.ai_summary: Email sent with pricing overview and comparison document highlighting our differentiation from Gong's conversation intelligence approach. No response yet.
Attempted follow-up call to confirm receipt of pricing document and schedule discovery. Left voicemail, no callback received.
LinkedIn message checking in on pricing document. Kwame responded saying he reviewed it and wants to move forward with discovery call.
Follow-up email to schedule discovery call. Kwame confirmed availability and mentioned bringing Ava Roberts from his team who handles technical evaluation. Positive tone.

questions_asked.question: Can we record the discovery call for our internal team review?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, we'll make sure to record and share it afterward.

next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for next week
Send calendar invite including Ava Roberts

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Kwame. Discussed detailed current state workflow, pain points around manual data entry and lack of visibility into onboarding progress. Kwame shared they have 24 sales reps who would use the system. He mentioned competitive evaluation with Gong ongoing.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for this project in 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Cutting onboarding time in half and having full visibility into every customer's progress without manual check-ins.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in the decision besides you and Ava?
questions_asked.answer: Ultimately our VP of Sales will need to sign off, but I'm driving this evaluation.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 2-4 weeks depending on integration complexity with your existing systems.

next_steps: Schedule demo focused on onboarding automation workflows
Prepare custom demo environment with EdTech use case

touches.ai_summary: Demo call with Kwame showing onboarding automation workflows. Walked through automated task assignment, progress tracking, and reporting dashboards. Kwame engaged throughout, asked specific questions about customization. He noted Gong offers some similar features but acknowledged our automation focus is stronger.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the workflow stages to match our specific onboarding process?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully customizable - we can map it exactly to your current stages and add automation rules at each step.

questions_asked.question: How does this integrate with our existing CRM?
questions_asked.answer: We have native integrations with all major CRMs - bi-directional sync, so data stays consistent across systems.

questions_asked.question: What would differentiate us from Gong in your evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, Gong's strength is conversation intelligence. You're purpose-built for the automation piece we need most.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation with Ava Roberts
Send demo recording and feature documentation

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation call with Ava Roberts. Deep dive into integration architecture, API capabilities, security compliance, and data handling. Ava asked detailed technical questions about scalability and our consulting services for implementation. She confirmed technical requirements are met but wants to see executive-level ROI analysis.

questions_asked.question: What's your API rate limit and can we build custom integrations if needed?
questions_asked.answer: Standard tier is 1000 requests/minute, and yes - full REST API documentation available for custom integrations.

questions_asked.question: Do you offer implementation consulting or is it self-service?
questions_asked.answer: We have consulting packages - for your use case we'd recommend the Automations Plus Consulting tier which includes dedicated implementation support.

questions_asked.question: How do you handle data residency and compliance for EdTech customers?
questions_asked.answer: SOC 2 Type II certified, GDPR compliant, and we can accommodate specific data residency requirements.

questions_asked.question: Any technical blockers you're seeing from your side?
questions_asked.answer: No blockers. We'd need to involve our security team for final review but nothing concerning so far.

objections: Need to understand the ROI model better before presenting to leadership
next_steps: Prepare ROI analysis based on their current metrics
Schedule executive presentation with VP of Sales

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Kwame present. Walked through business case, ROI projections showing $127k savings over 3 years from efficiency gains. Discussion focused on implementation timeline and resource requirements. Kwame asked about pricing but no VP of Sales present on the call despite agenda.

questions_asked.question: What's the total investment including consulting services?
questions_asked.answer: For 24 seats with Automations Plus Consulting tier, we're looking at $16,500 annual contract plus one-time implementation.

questions_asked.question: Is your VP of Sales available to join us to discuss the business case?
questions_asked.answer: She's tied up this week but I can present this to her. She trusts my recommendation on tools like this.

questions_asked.question: How does this pricing compare to what Gong quoted us?
questions_asked.answer: Gong's pricing model is typically higher for conversation intelligence - we can provide a detailed feature-price comparison.

next_steps: Kwame to present business case to VP of Sales
Follow up next week on executive feedback

touches.ai_summary: Second executive meeting, again with Kwame but no VP present. He mentioned VP reviewed the proposal and has concerns about price point given current budget constraints. Kwame pushing back on pricing, comparing heavily to Gong's quote. Discussion became focused on discount possibilities.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the pricing? Gong came in lower and we're budget-constrained this quarter.
questions_asked.answer: Let me talk to our team about what's possible. What's the budget range you're working within?

questions_asked.question: Can we start with fewer seats and scale up?
questions_asked.answer: We can discuss a phased approach, though the per-seat pricing would be higher with smaller initial commitment.

questions_asked.question: If we can get closer to your budget, when would you be ready to move forward?
questions_asked.answer: We need to make a decision by end of quarter. If pricing works, we can move quickly.

objections: Price point is higher than competitive option from Gong
next_steps: Explore discount options internally

touches.ai_summary: Rescheduled negotiation call finally occurred. Presented revised pricing with 35% discount to match budget constraints. Kwame receptive but still no VP engagement directly. He mentioned Gong is their other finalist and decision timeline is tight.

questions_asked.question: If we agree to this pricing, can we add more seats mid-year at the same rate?

questions_asked.question: Can we schedule time with your VP to finalize? I want to make sure we're addressing any remaining concerns directly.

objections: Still need to finalize comparison with Gong before committing

Still no direct VP involvement but Kwame indicated he has approval. Tone positive but noted he's managing the procurement process solo.
